title: Coordination Basics
description: Learn Same/Different relations with simple single-premise trials

trainingTrials.explanation: If GYQ is the same as FYW, then FYW is the same as GYQ (symmetry).
VOP is the SAME as CUB, not different.
If KES is different from DAX, then DAX is different from KES.
PIR is DIFFERENT from MOQ, not the same.
Same relation is symmetrical.

title: Derived Coordination
description: Derive Same relations through transitive chains (A=B, B=C → A=C)

trainingTrials.explanation: GYQ=FYW and FYW=VOP, so GYQ=VOP by transitivity.
CUB=KES=DAX, so DAX=CUB.
PIR=MOQ but MOQ≠ZUB, so PIR≠ZUB.
TEL=BEQ=RUF, so RUF is the same as TEL, not different.

title: Opposition
description: Learn Opposite relations and how they interact with Same

trainingTrials.explanation: Opposite is symmetrical: if A is opposite B, then B is opposite A.
VOP=CUB and CUB is opposite KES, so VOP is opposite KES.
DAX is opposite PIR, and PIR is opposite MOQ. Opposite of opposite = same.
ZUB is opposite TEL, not the same.

title: Comparison Basics
description: Learn More Than / Less Than relations

trainingTrials.explanation: If GYQ > FYW, then FYW < GYQ (inverse).
If VOP < CUB, then CUB > VOP.
KES > DAX, so DAX is NOT more than KES.
PIR < MOQ, so PIR is NOT more than MOQ.

title: Derived Comparison
description: Derive More/Less relations through transitive chains

trainingTrials.explanation: GYQ > FYW > VOP, so GYQ > VOP.
CUB > KES > DAX, so DAX is NOT more than CUB.
PIR < MOQ < ZUB, so PIR < ZUB.

title: Temporal Relations
description: Learn Before/After relations and derive temporal order

trainingTrials.explanation: If GYQ is before FYW, then FYW is after GYQ.
VOP → CUB → KES, so VOP is before KES.
MOQ → PIR → DAX, so MOQ is before DAX.

title: Mixed Relations I
description: Combine Same, Opposite, and Comparison relations

trainingTrials.explanation: GYQ=FYW and FYW>VOP, so GYQ>VOP.
CUB is opposite KES, and KES>DAX. Opposite reverses the relation, so CUB<DAX.
PIR=MOQ and MOQ<ZUB, so PIR<ZUB, not more than.

title: Triple Premise Chains
description: Derive relations across three-premise chains

trainingTrials.explanation: GYQ=FYW>VOP>CUB, so GYQ>CUB.
KES<DAX=PIR<MOQ, so KES<MOQ.
ZUB>TEL>BEQ=RUF, so RUF is NOT more than ZUB.

title: Containment Relations
description: Learn Contains/Within relations for hierarchical reasoning

trainingTrials.explanation: If GYQ contains FYW, then FYW is within GYQ.
If VOP is within CUB, then CUB contains VOP.
KES contains DAX, so DAX does NOT contain KES.
PIR is within MOQ, not the reverse.

title: Advanced Temporal Chains
description: Derive temporal order through three-premise chains

trainingTrials.explanation: GYQ then FYW then VOP then CUB, so GYQ is before CUB.
MOQ then PIR then DAX then KES, so MOQ is before KES.
ZUB then TEL then BEQ then RUF, so RUF is NOT before ZUB.

title: Multi-Frame Coordination
description: Combine Same, Different, and Opposite in multi-premise chains

trainingTrials.explanation: GYQ=FYW and FYW is different from VOP, so GYQ is different from VOP.
CUB is opposite KES, and KES=DAX, so CUB is opposite DAX.
Opposite of opposite = same.
TEL is different from BEQ, and BEQ=RUF, so TEL is different from RUF.

title: Complex Comparison Chains
description: Derive comparison relations across three-premise mixed chains

trainingTrials.explanation: GYQ>FYW=VOP>CUB, so GYQ>CUB.
KES<DAX<PIR=MOQ, so KES<MOQ.
ZUB=TEL>BEQ>RUF, so RUF is NOT more than ZUB.

title: Temporal-Comparison Mix
description: Combine temporal and comparison relations for cross-frame reasoning

trainingTrials.explanation: GYQ then FYW then VOP, so VOP is after GYQ.
CUB>KES>DAX, so DAX<CUB.
ZUB then MOQ then PIR, so ZUB is NOT after PIR.
TEL<BEQ<RUF, so TEL is NOT more than RUF.

title: Symmetric Relations Combined
description: Master all symmetric relations (Same, Different, Opposite) in complex chains

trainingTrials.explanation: GYQ=FYW, FYW is opposite VOP, VOP=CUB, so GYQ is opposite CUB.
Three opposites: opposite(opposite(opposite))=opposite. KES is opposite MOQ.
ZUB=TEL=BEQ, and BEQ is opposite RUF, so ZUB is opposite RUF, not same.

title: Containment Chains
description: Derive containment relations through transitive chains

trainingTrials.explanation: GYQ contains FYW, FYW contains VOP, so GYQ contains VOP.
CUB is within KES, KES is within DAX, so CUB is within DAX.
PIR=MOQ and MOQ contains ZUB, so PIR contains ZUB.
TEL is within BEQ, BEQ is within RUF, so RUF is NOT within TEL.

title: Four-Premise Comparison
description: Derive comparison across four-premise chains for expert reasoning

trainingTrials.explanation: GYQ>FYW>VOP=CUB>KES, so GYQ>KES.
DAX<PIR=MOQ<ZUB<TEL, so TEL is NOT less than DAX.
BEQ>RUF>WEX>JOT=NAV, so NAV<BEQ.

title: Inverse Derivation
description: Focus on deriving inverse and reciprocal relations

trainingTrials.explanation: GYQ>FYW>VOP, so VOP<GYQ.
CUB then KES then DAX, so DAX is after CUB.
PIR contains MOQ, MOQ contains ZUB, so ZUB is within PIR.
RUF then BEQ then TEL, so RUF is BEFORE TEL, not after.

title: Speed Challenge
description: Faster time limits push fluency with all relation types

title: Mixed Hard Chains
description: Three-premise chains mixing all relation types

trainingTrials.explanation: GYQ=FYW, FYW is opposite VOP, and VOP>CUB. Opposite reverses: GYQ<CUB.
KES contains DAX, DAX=PIR, PIR contains MOQ, so KES contains MOQ.
ZUB then TEL=BEQ then RUF, so RUF is after ZUB.
WEX>JOT>NAV=QIP, so QIP is NOT more than WEX.

title: Expert Challenge
description: Four-premise chains with all relation types for mastery-level reasoning

trainingTrials.explanation: GYQ>FYW=VOP>CUB>KES, so GYQ>KES.
DAX then PIR=MOQ then ZUB then TEL, so TEL is after DAX.
BEQ contains RUF=WEX which contains JOT which contains NAV, so NAV is within BEQ.
QIP is opposite DUK=FEZ, FEZ is opposite LYR=HEW. Two opposites cancel: QIP=HEW.
